pages.text: One sunny morning, <name> found a shiny golden ladoo on the kitchen table. He picked it up and clapped his hands — “CLAP!” — and the ladoo sprang into the air!

“Wow! You can fly!” <name> cheered. He clapped again, and the ladoo twirled around the room like a tiny golden helicopter. The cat tried to catch it, jumping and missing, while the dog barked happily.

<name> wanted to take the ladoo outside, but oops! It flew too high and landed on the rooftop. “Oh no!” he giggled. He ran to get a chair and tiptoed carefully, but the ladoo spun off the roof and landed softly in a flower pot.
He clapped gently, thinking, Maybe I need to call it down nicely. The ladoo floated down slowly, landing safely in <name>’s hands.
“Phew! That was a high-flying adventure!” <name> laughed, brushing petals off the ladoo. He decided to keep it simple now, clapping only once or twice, letting the ladoo hover gently around him.

Finally, he sat in the garden, took a tiny bite, and savored the sweet treat. “You’re the most magical ladoo ever!” he said, eyes sparkling. <name> spent the afternoon clapping softly and giggling as the ladoo floated nearby.
